senses.definition: a part of a road that is separated by white lines

examples.x: He switched to the left lane to overtake the slow-moving truck.
She signaled before merging into the right lane.
The construction work narrowed the highway to one lane.
Drivers must stay within their lane to ensure safe and orderly traffic flow.

senses.definition: the lane on a multi-lane road designated for vehicles traveling at slower speeds

examples.x: He moved into the slow lane to let faster cars pass.
She preferred driving in the slow lane during heavy rain.
The truck stayed in the slow lane while climbing the hill.
They merged from the highway into the slow lane.
The slow lane was clear of traffic early in the morning.

senses.definition: the lane on a multi-lane road designated for vehicles traveling at higher speeds

examples.x: She overtook the slower car by moving into the fast lane.
The sports car sped down the fast lane with ease.
He signaled before switching to the fast lane.
They avoided the fast lane due to heavy traffic.
The fast lane was reserved for passing only.

senses.definition: the lane on a road or highway that vehicles use to overtake slower-moving traffic

examples.x: Drivers should use the passing lane only when they need to overtake slower vehicles ahead of them.
It's important to keep the passing lane clear for faster-moving traffic to maintain a smooth flow of vehicles.
In some countries, there are strict rules about how long you can stay in the passing lane if you're not overtaking another vehicle.
When approaching a slower vehicle, it's courteous to move into the passing lane briefly to overtake and then return to the right lane.
Using the passing lane responsibly helps prevent congestion and promotes safer driving habits on busy highways.

senses.definition: a designated highway lane reserved for vehicles carrying multiple occupants, typically with a minimum requirement of two or more people

examples.x: Using the HOV lane during rush hour can help commuters avoid heavy traffic.
Drivers must adhere to the posted signs to determine when they can enter the HOV lane.
Carpooling is encouraged to make the most efficient use of the HOV lane.
Violators of HOV lane rules may face fines or penalties.
The HOV lane is intended to promote more efficient transportation by reducing congestion on major roadways.

senses.definition: a designated highway lane where drivers can choose to pay a toll for faster travel
a traffic lane that changes direction based on the time of day or traffic flow

examples.x: In some cities, reversible lanes are used during rush hours to accommodate the heavy influx of commuters heading in one direction.
Drivers must pay close attention to the signs indicating when a reversible lane is in effect to avoid driving against traffic.
It is important for motorists to adhere to the posted speed limits and lane markings when using reversible lanes to ensure safety for all road users.
Before traveling, it's wise to check local traffic reports to understand when reversible lanes might be active on your route.
Authorities regularly monitor reversible lanes to adjust their operations based on traffic conditions and improve overall traffic flow.

senses.definition: an additional lane on a road designed to help slower vehicles move uphill without impeding faster traffic

examples.x: The highway has a climbing lane that allows trucks to ascend the steep hill without slowing down other vehicles.
Drivers appreciate climbing lanes because they reduce congestion during peak travel times.
When approaching a climbing lane, motorists should check for merging vehicles to ensure safe lane changes.
A climbing lane typically appears on roads with frequent elevation changes to improve traffic flow.
During construction, workers added a climbing lane to ease traffic congestion caused by slow-moving vehicles.

senses.definition: a designated part of the road marked specifically for cyclists to ride safely

examples.x: In many cities, bicycle lanes are painted with white lines to separate them from car traffic.
Cyclists appreciate bicycle lanes because they provide a safer route through busy areas.
It's important for drivers to be aware of bicycle lanes and yield to cyclists when turning across them.
Some bicycle lanes are physically separated from car lanes by barriers or curbs for added safety.
City planners often consider adding bicycle lanes to improve transportation options and reduce traffic congestion.

senses.definition: a sharp bend or turn in a road

examples.x: The road had a dangerous dogleg near the end.
She slowed down to navigate the dogleg safely.
He almost missed the turn at the sudden dogleg.
The dogleg made the route more challenging for drivers.
They installed warning signs before the dogleg.

senses.definition: a designated area on a road for cyclists

examples.x: In many cities, bike lanes are marked with painted symbols of bicycles to indicate where cyclists should ride.
The city council plans to expand the bike lane network to encourage more people to cycle instead of drive.
It is important for drivers to be aware of bike lanes and yield to cyclists when turning across them.
During rush hour, the bike lane can be quite busy with commuters on bicycles heading to work or school.
Safety precautions such as wearing helmets and using lights at night are recommended for cyclists using bike lanes.

senses.definition: a lane on the side of a highway where vehicles can stop in case of emergency or mechanical failure

examples.x: The car pulled over into the breakdown lane after a flat tire.
She called for help from the breakdown lane.
The breakdown lane was clear of obstructions.
They towed the broken-down vehicle from the breakdown lane.
The breakdown lane provided a safe space to fix minor issues.

senses.definition: the part of a road where vehicles can go faster due to light traffic there

examples.x: I took the express lane to avoid the heavy traffic on the main highway.
If you're in a hurry, you can pay to use the express lane and bypass all the cars.
The express lane is a great way to save time during rush hour.
I always try to get into the express lane when I'm late for work.
The new express lane has made my daily commute so much smoother.

senses.definition: a traffic lane reserved for vehicles with a minimum number of passengers, typically to encourage carpooling and reduce congestion

examples.x: During rush hour, the carpool lane moves much faster than the regular lanes because it's reserved for vehicles with at least two passengers.
We decided to share a ride to work so we could use the carpool lane and avoid the heavy traffic on the highway.
If you get caught driving solo in the carpool lane, you'll face a hefty fine for violating the carpool regulations.
Electric vehicles and motorcycles are sometimes allowed to use the carpool lane even without additional passengers.
To promote environmental sustainability, many cities are expanding their carpool lane networks to encourage more people to carpool.

senses.definition: the raised edge at the side of a street, usually made of stone

examples.x: The city installed a new curb to improve pedestrian safety.
The construction crew repaired the damaged curb after the winter storms.
A high curb can pose a challenge for people with mobility issues.
The curb along the street was painted to enhance visibility at night.

senses.definition: the area adjacent to the edge of a street or road where vehicles can park or where services, such as deliveries or pickups, often take place

examples.x: He waited at the curbside for the taxi to arrive.
Residents were instructed to place their trash bins at the curbside for collection.
The delivery truck parked at the curbside to unload the packages.

senses.definition: the slight curve or tilt of a road surface to help with drainage and stability

examples.x: The road's camber directed rainwater to the sides.
She noticed the camber while driving along the curved road.
He adjusted his speed to account for the road's camber.
They designed the road with a gentle camber for safety.
The camber helped prevent water from pooling on the road.

senses.definition: a curve in a road, river, etc.

examples.x: The car approached a sharp bend in the road and slowed down.
The river took a meandering bend through the picturesque valley.

senses.definition: a sharp turn or bend in a road or trail that zigzags in order to manage a steep incline or decline

examples.x: The hikers followed the switchback trail up the mountain to reach the summit.
The road to the remote village was filled with switchbacks, making the journey slow but scenic.
The engineer designed the mountain highway with numerous switchbacks to ensure safe passage for vehicles.
Climbing the switchback path was challenging, but the breathtaking views were worth the effort.
The tour guide led the group down a series of switchbacks, explaining how they were built to minimize the grade of the trail.

senses.definition: the degree of the angle between the ground and the sloping line, road, etc.
a sharp bend in a road that sharply changes its direction

examples.x: The mountain road had several hairpin turns, making it challenging for drivers to navigate.
She gripped the steering wheel tightly as she approached the hairpin turn on the narrow road.
The cyclist slowed down to safely maneuver the hairpin turn at the bottom of the hill.
The bus driver skillfully negotiated the hairpin turn despite the vehicle's large size.
Signs warned drivers of the upcoming hairpin turn ahead, advising them to reduce speed.

senses.definition: having multiple twists and turns

examples.x: They took a stroll along the winding river path.
The winding staircase led to the top of the tower.

senses.definition: a circular intersection where multiple roads meet

examples.x: She entered the circle and took the third exit.
The new circle reduced traffic congestion at the intersection.
He yielded to traffic already in the circle.
They added a landscaped island in the center of the circle.
The circle improved the flow of vehicles through the area.

senses.definition: a circuitous path or route that returns to the starting point

examples.x: The hiking trail formed a loop around the lake, offering picturesque views from all angles.
We took a loop around the city to get a better sense of its layout before settling on a place to eat.
The bus tour took us on a loop around the historic district, stopping at significant landmarks along the way.
After getting lost in the maze, we finally found the correct path that led us in a loop around the garden.
The marathon course was designed to loop around the park twice, showcasing its natural beauty to the runners.

senses.definition: the point where two or more roads or lanes come together and traffic must combine

examples.x: Drivers should use caution when approaching the highway merge to avoid accidents.
The new interchange design aims to reduce congestion at the busy merge.
At the merge, vehicles must yield to those already on the main road.
Traffic signs provide clear instructions on how to navigate the merge safely.
The construction project includes widening the merge to accommodate increased traffic flow.

senses.definition: a road feature designed to redirect traffic from a side road to make a safer left turn

examples.x: When driving in unfamiliar areas, drivers often encounter jughandles that lead them to make right turns to eventually go left.
The traffic engineer explained that jughandles are particularly useful in reducing left-turn accidents at busy intersections.
At the intersection, drivers must follow the jughandle to reach the highway entrance ramp safely.
Some drivers find jughandles confusing at first, but they quickly learn how to navigate them to reach their destination.
The new road construction includes several jughandles to streamline traffic flow and enhance safety for all drivers.

senses.definition: the area along the edge of a road

examples.x: The car stopped at the roadside to let passengers out.
She picked flowers growing by the roadside.
He saw a deer standing at the roadside.
They installed lights along the roadside for better visibility.
The roadside was littered with debris after the storm.

senses.definition: a narrow area of land or barrier that separates lanes of traffic on a highway or road

examples.x: The highway was divided by a median strip, with flowers planted along its length.
Drivers should be cautious when crossing the median strip to avoid accidents.
The workers installed new lights along the median strip to improve visibility at night.
The accident occurred when a vehicle veered across the median strip into oncoming traffic.
Pedestrians should never attempt to walk across the median strip due to the high-speed traffic nearby.

senses.definition: the strip of land bordering a road, often covered with grass or vegetation

examples.x: She walked her dog along the grassy verge.
He mowed the verge as part of his landscaping job.
They planted trees on the verge to improve aesthetics.
The car veered onto the verge to avoid a collision.
The verge provided a safe space for pedestrians away from traffic.

senses.definition: the strip of land on the side of a road where vehicles can stop in an emergency

examples.x: The driver pulled over onto the shoulder to check the engine.
She parked on the shoulder to make a phone call.
He saw a stranded vehicle on the shoulder ahead.
They expanded the shoulder to allow more space for emergency stops.
The shoulder was used by cyclists and pedestrians in some areas.

senses.definition: the unpaved or less stable portion alongside a road, typically made of gravel, dirt, or grass, designated for emergency stopping or vehicle breakdowns

examples.x: Drivers should be cautious when pulling onto the soft shoulder to avoid getting stuck in mud or gravel.
If your car breaks down on the highway, it's safer to park on the soft shoulder rather than in a traffic lane.
The police officer directed the driver to move his vehicle onto the soft shoulder after the minor accident.
Signs along the highway remind motorists not to litter on the soft shoulder to maintain cleanliness and safety.
During heavy rain, the soft shoulder can become muddy and difficult to navigate, so drivers are advised to use caution.

senses.definition: a tall metal structure used for carrying high-voltage power lines above the ground

examples.x: The transmission lines were supported by tall pylons across the countryside.
Engineers inspected the pylon for structural integrity after a severe storm.
The new housing development was planned around existing electricity pylons.
Pylons are designed to withstand strong winds and other environmental conditions.
The power company erected additional pylons to meet growing electricity demands in the region.

senses.definition: a structure spanning over a road that supports signs, signals, or cameras

examples.x: The gantry displayed the speed limit for each lane.
He noticed a camera mounted on the gantry.
The gantry was installed to hold the new electronic toll signs.
She saw the traffic lights hanging from the gantry.
They inspected the gantry for structural integrity.

senses.definition: a big sign used for advertising, usually found near roads or highways

examples.x: The billboard advertised the latest smartphone on the highway.
A bright billboard caught his attention while driving.
The company rented a billboard to promote their new product.
She saw her favorite celebrity on a giant billboard downtown.
The billboard displayed a message about road safety.

senses.definition: a place where something becomes narrow, often causing a restriction or bottleneck

examples.x: Traffic often builds up at pinch points like bridges and tunnels during rush hour.
The meeting room's small size created a pinch point for accommodating all the attendees comfortably.
In project management, identifying potential pinch points early helps in smoother execution of plans.
The team faced a pinch point in their workflow when the printer broke down unexpectedly.
Adequate staffing is crucial to avoid pinch points in customer service during peak times.

senses.definition: a designated area where vehicles can reverse direction

examples.x: The driver used the turnaround to head back the way he came.
She missed the exit and had to find a turnaround.
He appreciated the well-marked turnaround on the highway.
They built a new turnaround to improve traffic flow.
The turnaround allowed large trucks to change direction safely.

senses.definition: (of a path or road) to split into another direction, creating a separate route

examples.x: The highway branches off into smaller roads leading to nearby towns.
As you hike, notice where the trail branches off into alternative paths.
The highway branches off into smaller roads leading to nearby towns.
The highway branches off near the mountain range, leading to picturesque routes.

senses.definition: to split into two or more separate paths or divisions

examples.x: The hiking trail forked, allowing hikers to choose between different routes.
The river forked into two smaller streams as it flowed through the valley.
The underground tunnels forked, leading to different sections of the ancient city.
In the road network, many intersections fork, offering various directions.

senses.definition: of a road or path that is clear and safe to travel on

examples.x: The road was barely passable after the heavy snowfall.
She checked if the mountain pass was passable before starting the trip.
He ensured the trail was passable for hikers.
They worked overnight to make the flooded road passable again.
The route remained passable despite the recent storms.

senses.definition: (of a path) not possible to travel across or through
the place where two or more streets, roads, etc. cross each other

examples.x: The traffic light at the intersection changed to red, stopping cars from all directions.
They waited for the green light at the busy intersection to continue their journey.
The city planned to redesign the intersection to improve safety and reduce congestion.
He noticed a new restaurant opening at the corner of the intersection where several roads converged.
She was involved in a minor accident at the intersection due to another driver running a red light.

senses.definition: a situation where two or more numbered highways overlap along the same stretch of road but are signed with opposing cardinal directions

examples.x: A wrong-way concurrency exists where US 1 South and US 29 North share the same roadway in Virginia.
Wrong-way concurrencies often arise when older routes are grandfathered into newer highway systems without directional adjustments.
Transportation departments try to eliminate wrong-way concurrencies because they increase the risk of driver confusion.
In Ontario, Highway 7 and Highway 8 once had a wrong-way concurrency near Kitchener, signed eastbound and westbound simultaneously.
The wrong-way concurrency on US 52 South and NC 8 North in North Carolina was eventually resolved by renumbering NC 8.
